revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 日枝神社日本橋摂社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=東京都中央区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]]中央区 (東京都)|中…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 日枝神社日本橋摂社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=東京都中央区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]][[日本橋茅場町]]1-6-16 |地図 = |祭神 = 日枝大神（[[大山咋神|大山咋神]]、[[国之常立神|国常立神]]、[[イザナミ|伊弉冉神]]、[[仲哀天皇|足仲彦尊]]） |社格 = |創建 = [[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 日本橋日枝神社、山王御旅所 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''日枝神社日本橋摂社'''（ひえじんじゃにほんばいせっしゃ）は、[[東京都]][[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]]の[[神社]]。[[千代田区]][[永田町]]の[[日枝神社 (千代田区)|日枝神社]]の境外[[摂末社|摂社]]である。
== 概要 == [[1590年]]（[[天正]]18年）、[[徳川家康]]の江戸入府に伴い、日枝神社の[[神輿]]が当地に安置されたのが起源である。安置された場所のことを「御旅所（おたびしょ）」という。現在でも隔年で開催される山王祭では、本社と当社間を神輿や[[山車]]が巡幸している<ref name="日本橋摂社">[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php 御由緒・御祭神]日枝神社日本橋摂社</ref><ref name="史跡散歩">金山正好、金山るみ 著『中央区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド2)』[[学生社]]、1993年、63p</ref>。
[[江戸時代]]は、[[智泉院 (東京都中央区)|智泉院]]が当社の[[別当寺]]であったが、[[明治時代]]の[[神仏分離]]により、当社から切り離された。そして本社日枝神社の社格上昇に伴い、当社も整備されていった<ref name="日本橋摂社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[茅場町駅]]より徒歩1分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *金山正好、金山るみ 著『中央区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド2)』[[学生社]]、1993年
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ 日本橋日枝神社]
{{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ひえしんしやにほんはしせつしや}} [[Category:東京都区部の神社]] [[Category:東京都中央区の建築物]] [[Category:東京都中央区の歴史]]

revision.comment: 画像を追加。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 日枝神社日本橋摂社 |画像 = [[File:山王日枝神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[東京都]][[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]][[日本橋茅場町]]1-6-16 |地図 = |祭神 = 日枝大神（[[大山咋神|大山咋神]]、[[国之常立神|国常立神]]、[[イザナミ|伊弉冉神]]、[[仲哀天皇|足仲彦尊]]） |社格 = |創建 = [[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 日本橋日枝神社、山王御旅所 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''日枝神社日本橋摂社'''（ひえじんじゃにほんばいせっしゃ）は、[[東京都]][[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]]の[[神社]]。[[千代田区]][[永田町]]の[[日枝神社 (千代田区)|日枝神社]]の境外[[摂末社|摂社]]である。
== 概要 == [[1590年]]（[[天正]]18年）、[[徳川家康]]の江戸入府に伴い、日枝神社の[[神輿]]が当地に安置されたのが起源である。安置された場所のことを「御旅所（おたびしょ）」という。現在でも隔年で開催される山王祭では、本社と当社間を神輿や[[山車]]が巡幸している<ref name="日本橋摂社">[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php 御由緒・御祭神]日枝神社日本橋摂社</ref><ref name="史跡散歩">金山正好、金山るみ 著『中央区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド2)』[[学生社]]、1993年、63p</ref>。
[[江戸時代]]は、[[智泉院 (東京都中央区)|智泉院]]が当社の[[別当寺]]であったが、[[明治時代]]の[[神仏分離]]により、当社から切り離された。そして本社日枝神社の社格上昇に伴い、当社も整備されていった<ref name="日本橋摂社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[茅場町駅]]より徒歩1分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *金山正好、金山るみ 著『中央区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド2)』[[学生社]]、1993年
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ 日本橋日枝神社]

{{神社 |名称 = 日枝神社日本橋摂社 |画像 = [[File:山王日枝神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]][[日本橋茅場町]]1-6-16 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 日枝大神（[[大山咋神]]、[[国之常立神|国常立神]]、[[イザナミ|伊弉冉神]]、[[仲哀天皇|足仲彦尊]]） |社格 = |創建 = [[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 日本橋日枝神社、山王御旅所 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''日枝神社日本橋摂社'''（ひえじんじゃにほんばしせっしゃ）は、[[東京都]][[中央区 (東京都)|中央区]]の[[神社]]。[[千代田区]][[永田町]]の[[日枝神社 (千代田区)|日枝神社]]の境外[[摂末社|摂社]]である。'''日本橋日枝神社'''（にほんばしひえじんじゃ）とも称される。 == 歴史 == [[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]）、[[徳川家康]]の江戸入府に伴い、日枝神社の[[神輿]]が当地に安置されたのが起源である。安置された場所のことを「[[御旅所]]（おたびしょ）」という。現在でも隔年で開催される山王祭では、本社と当社間を神輿や[[山車]]が巡幸している[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php 御由緒・御祭神]日枝神社日本橋摂社金山正好、金山るみ 著『中央区史跡散歩（東京史跡ガイド2）』[[学生社]]、1993年、63p。 [[江戸時代]]は、[[智泉院 (東京都中央区)|智泉院]]が当社の[[別当寺]]であったが、[[明治時代]]の[[神仏分離]]により、当社から切り離された。そして本社日枝神社の社格上昇に伴い、当社も整備されていった。 == 交通アクセス == *[[茅場町駅]]より徒歩1分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *金山正好、金山るみ 著『中央区史跡散歩（東京史跡ガイド2）』[[学生社]]、1993年 ==外部リンク== * [http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ 日本橋日枝神社] - 公式サイト * {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|日本橋日枝神社}} {{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ひえしんしやにほんはしせつしや}} [[Category:東京都区部の神社]] [[Category:東京都中央区の建築物]] [[Category:東京都中央区の歴史]] [[Category:日本橋 (東京都中央区)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Nihonbashi Hie Shrine |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = [[File:山王日枝神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E}}}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|qq=}}1-6-16 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Deity = 日枝大神（[[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakui-no-kami]]、[[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi-no-kami]]、[[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]、[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Ashinakahiko-no-mikoto]]） |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |Founded = [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year （{{ill|1590|ja|1590|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1590|qq=}}） |Main shrine = |Also known as = Nihonbashi Hie Shrine, Sannō Otabi-sho |Fuda sites etc = |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine''' (日枝神社日本橋摂社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|qq=}}. It is the external [[:en:Setsumatsusha|Sessha]] of the [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]] in [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda Ward]] [[:en:Nagatachō|Nagatachō]]. It is also called '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine'''. ==History== In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1590|qq=}}), following the entry of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] into Edo, the [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable Shrine]] of Hie Shrine was enshrined here. The location of the enshrinement is called [[:en:Otabisho|Otabi-sho]]. Even today, during the Sannō Festival held every other year, the portable shrine and [[:en:Matsuri float|Dashi]] make a tour between the main shrine and this shrine[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php Venerable history and enshrined deities] Nihonbashi Hie Shrine{{Sfn|Masayoshi Kanayama|Rumi Kanayama|1993|p=63}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], {{ill|Chisenin|ja|智泉院 (東京都中央区)|lt=Chisenin|lt_ja=智泉院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chisenin|qq=}} served as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|qq=}}) of this shrine; however, in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), it was detached from this shrine. Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *[[:en:Kayabachō Station|Kayabacho Station]] (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|qq=}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Nihonbashi Hie Shrine]] [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] [[Category:日吉神社]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都中央区の神社]]

拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E}}}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|qq=}}1-6-16 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Deity = 日枝大神（[[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakui-no-kami]]、[[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi-no-kami]]、[[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]、[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Ashinakahiko-no-mikoto]]） |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |Founded = [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year （{{ill|1590|ja|1590|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1590|qq=}}） |Main shrine = |Also known as = Nihonbashi Hie Shrine, Sannō Otabi-sho |Fuda sites etc = |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine''' (日枝神社日本橋摂社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|qq=}}. It is the external [[:en:Setsumatsusha|Sessha]] of the [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]] in [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda Ward]] [[:en:Nagatachō|Nagatachō]]. It is also called '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine'''. ==History== In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1590|qq=}}), following the entry of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] into Edo, the [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable Shrine]] of Hie Shrine was enshrined here. The location of the enshrinement is called [[:en:Otabisho|Otabi-sho]]. Even today, during the Sannō Festival held every other year, the portable shrine and [[:en:Matsuri float|Dashi]] make a tour between the main shrine and this shrine[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php Venerable history and enshrined deities] Nihonbashi Hie Shrine{{Sfn|Masayoshi Kanayama|Rumi Kanayama|1993|p=63}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], {{ill|Chisenin|ja|智泉院 (東京都中央区)|lt=Chisenin|lt_ja=智泉院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chisenin|qq=}} served as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|qq=}}) of this shrine; however, in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), it was detached from this shrine. Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *[[:en:Kayabachō Station|Kayabacho Station]] (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|qq=}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Nihonbashi Hie Shrine]] [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] [[Category:日吉神社]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都中央区の神社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E}}}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|qq=}}1-6-16 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Deity = 日枝大神（[[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakui-no-kami]]、[[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi-no-kami]]、[[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]、[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Ashinakahiko-no-mikoto]]） |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |Founded = [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year （{{ill|1590|ja|1590|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1590|qq=}}） |Main shrine = |Also known as = Nihonbashi Hie Shrine, Sannō Otabi-sho |Fuda sites etc = |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine''' (日枝神社日本橋摂社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|qq=}}. It is the external [[:en:Setsumatsusha|Sessha]] of the [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]] in [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda Ward]] [[:en:Nagatachō|Nagatachō]]. It is also called '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine'''. ==History== In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1590|qq=}}), following the entry of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] into Edo, the [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable Shrine]] of Hie Shrine was enshrined here. The location of the enshrinement is called [[:en:Otabisho|Otabi-sho]]. Even today, during the Sannō Festival held every other year, the portable shrine and [[:en:Matsuri float|Dashi]] make a tour between the main shrine and this shrine[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php Venerable history and enshrined deities] Nihonbashi Hie Shrine{{Sfn|Masayoshi Kanayama|Rumi Kanayama|1993|p=63}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], {{ill|Chisenin|ja|智泉院 (東京都中央区)|lt=Chisenin|lt_ja=智泉院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chisenin|qq=}} served as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|qq=}}) of this shrine; however, in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), it was detached from this shrine. Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *[[:en:Kayabachō Station|Kayabacho Station]] (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|qq=}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Nihonbashi Hie Shrine]] [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] [[Category:Sannō-jinja]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都中央区の神社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E}}}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|qq=}}1-6-16 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Deity = 日枝大神（[[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakui-no-kami]]、[[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi-no-kami]]、[[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]、[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Ashinakahiko-no-mikoto]]） |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |Founded = [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year （[[:en:1590|1590]]） |Main shrine = |Also known as = Nihonbashi Hie Shrine, Sannō Otabi-sho |Fuda sites etc = |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine''' (日枝神社日本橋摂社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|qq=}}. It is the external [[:en:Setsumatsusha|Sessha]] of the [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]] in [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda Ward]] [[:en:Nagatachō|Nagatachō]]. It is also called '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine'''. ==History== In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year ([[:en:1590|1590]]), following the entry of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] into Edo, the [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable Shrine]] of Hie Shrine was enshrined here. The location of the enshrinement is called [[:en:Otabisho|Otabi-sho]]. Even today, during the Sannō Festival held every other year, the portable shrine and [[:en:Matsuri float|Dashi]] make a tour between the main shrine and this shrine[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php Venerable history and enshrined deities] Nihonbashi Hie Shrine{{Sfn|Masayoshi Kanayama|Rumi Kanayama|1993|p=63}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], {{ill|Chisenin|ja|智泉院 (東京都中央区)|lt=Chisenin|lt_ja=智泉院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chisenin|qq=}} served as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) of this shrine; however, in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), it was detached from this shrine. Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *[[:en:Kayabachō Station|Kayabacho Station]] (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|qq=}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Nihonbashi Hie Shrine]] [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] [[Category:Sannō-jinja]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E}}}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|qq=|1=Nihonbashi-Kayabachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508343}}1-6-16 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Deity = 日枝大神（[[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakui-no-kami]]、[[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi-no-kami]]、[[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]、[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Ashinakahiko-no-mikoto]]） |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |Founded = [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year （[[:en:1590|1590]]） |Main shrine = |Also known as = Nihonbashi Hie Shrine, Sannō Otabi-sho |Fuda sites etc = |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine''' (日枝神社日本橋摂社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|qq=|1=Nihonbashi-Kayabachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508343}}. It is the external [[:en:Setsumatsusha|Sessha]] of the [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]] in [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda Ward]] [[:en:Nagatachō|Nagatachō]]. It is also called '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine'''. ==History== In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year ([[:en:1590|1590]]), following the entry of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] into Edo, the [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable Shrine]] of Hie Shrine was enshrined here. The location of the enshrinement is called [[:en:Otabisho|Otabi-sho]]. Even today, during the Sannō Festival held every other year, the portable shrine and [[:en:Matsuri float|Dashi]] make a tour between the main shrine and this shrine[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php Venerable history and enshrined deities] Nihonbashi Hie Shrine{{Sfn|Masayoshi Kanayama|Rumi Kanayama|1993|p=63}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], {{ill|Chisenin|ja|智泉院 (東京都中央区)|lt=Chisenin|lt_ja=智泉院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chisenin|qq=|1=Chisenin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106836711}} served as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) of this shrine; however, in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), it was detached from this shrine. Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *[[:en:Kayabachō Station|Kayabacho Station]] (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|qq=|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Nihonbashi Hie Shrine]] [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] [[Category:Sannō-jinja]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]]

Even today, during the Sannō Festival held every other year, the portable shrine and [[:en:Matsuri float|Dashi]] make a tour between the main shrine and this shrine[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php Venerable history and enshrined deities] Nihonbashi Hie Shrine{{Sfn|Masayoshi Kanayama|Rumi Kanayama|1993|p=63}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], {{ill|Chisenin|ja|智泉院 (東京都中央区)|lt=Chisenin|lt_ja=智泉院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chisenin|qq=|1=Chisen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106836711}} served as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) of this shrine; however, in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), it was detached from this shrine. Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *[[:en:Kayabachō Station|Kayabacho Station]] (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|qq=|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] [[Category:Sannō Shrines]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] {{wikidata link|Q106852387}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E}}}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|1=Nihonbashi-Kayabachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508343}}1-6-16 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Deity = 日枝大神（[[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakui-no-kami]]、[[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi-no-kami]]、[[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]、[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Ashinakahiko-no-mikoto]]） |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |Founded = [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year （[[:en:1590|1590]]） |Main shrine = |Also known as = Nihonbashi Hie Shrine, Sannō Otabi-sho |Fuda sites etc = |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine''' (日枝神社日本橋摂社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|1=Nihonbashi-Kayabachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508343}}. It is the external [[:en:Setsumatsusha|Sessha]] of the [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]] in [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda Ward]] [[:en:Nagatachō|Nagatachō]]. It is also called '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine'''. ==History== In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year ([[:en:1590|1590]]), following the entry of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] into Edo, the [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable Shrine]] of Hie Shrine was enshrined here. The location of the enshrinement is called [[:en:Otabisho|Otabi-sho]]. Even today, during the Sannō Festival held every other year, the portable shrine and [[:en:Matsuri float|Dashi]] make a tour between the main shrine and this shrine[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php Venerable history and enshrined deities] Nihonbashi Hie Shrine{{Sfn|Masayoshi Kanayama|Rumi Kanayama|1993|p=63}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], {{ill|Chisenin|ja|智泉院 (東京都中央区)|lt=Chisenin|lt_ja=智泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chisenin|1=Chisen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106836711}} served as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) of this shrine; however, in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), it was detached from this shrine. Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *[[:en:Kayabachō Station|Kayabacho Station]] (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] [[Category:Sannō Shrines]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] {{wikidata link|Q106852387}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Even today, during the Sannō Festival held every other year, the portable shrine and [[:en:Matsuri float|Dashi]] make a tour between the main shrine and this shrine[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php Venerable history and enshrined deities] Nihonbashi Hie Shrine{{Sfn|Masayoshi Kanayama|Rumi Kanayama|1993|p=63}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], {{ill|Chisenin|ja|智泉院 (東京都中央区)|lt=Chisenin|lt_ja=智泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chisenin|1=Chisen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106836711}} served as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) of this shrine; however, in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), it was detached from this shrine. Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *[[:en:Kayabachō Station|Kayabacho Station]] (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] [[Category:Sannō Shrines]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] {{wikidata link|Q106852387}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852387]] Wikidata name: Nihonbashi Hie Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Nihonbashi Hie Shrine |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = [[File:山王日枝神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E}}}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|1=Nihonbashi-Kayabachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508343}}1-6-16 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Deity = 日枝大神（[[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamakui-no-kami]]、[[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi-no-kami]]、[[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]、[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Ashinakahiko-no-mikoto]]） |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |Founded = [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year （[[:en:1590|1590]]） |Main shrine = |Also known as = Nihonbashi Hie Shrine, Sannō Otabi-sho |Fuda sites etc = |Religious affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine''' (日枝神社日本橋摂社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] [[:en:Chūō, Tokyo|Chūō]] {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|1=Nihonbashi-Kayabachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508343}}. It is the external [[:en:Setsumatsusha|Sessha]] of the [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]] in [[:en:Chiyoda, Tokyo|Chiyoda Ward]] [[:en:Nagatachō|Nagatachō]]. It is also called '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine'''. ==History== In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]]18th year ([[:en:1590|1590]]), following the entry of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] into Edo, the [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable Shrine]] of Hie Shrine was enshrined here. The location of the enshrinement is called [[:en:Otabisho|Otabi-sho]]. Even today, during the Sannō Festival held every other year, the portable shrine and [[:en:Matsuri float|Dashi]] make a tour between the main shrine and this shrine[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php Venerable history and enshrined deities] Nihonbashi Hie Shrine{{Sfn|Masayoshi Kanayama|Rumi Kanayama|1993|p=63}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], {{ill|Chisenin|ja|智泉院 (東京都中央区)|lt=Chisenin|lt_ja=智泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chisenin|1=Chisen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106836711}} served as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) of this shrine; however, in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]), it was detached from this shrine. Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *[[:en:Kayabachō Station|Kayabacho Station]] (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852387}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sannō Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *{{ill|Kayabachō Station|en|Kayabachō Station|WD=Q80566|lt=Kayabacho Station}} (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852387}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sannō Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: Removing {{afc comment}} templates
revision.text:
[[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Nihonbashi Hie Shrine |Religious affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |image = [[File:山王日枝神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E}}}} |Religious affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |Location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}} {{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|en|Chūō, Tokyo|WD=Q212704|lt=Chūō}} {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|1=Nihonbashi-Kayabachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508343}}1-6-16 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|40|51.5|N|139|46|44.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Religious affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |Deity = 日枝大神（{{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-kami}}、{{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuninotokotachi-no-kami}}、{{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}}、{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Ashinakahiko-no-mikoto}}） |Religious affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |type = |Founded = {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}}18th year （{{ill|1590|en|1590|WD=Q6764|lt=1590}}） |Main shrine = |Also known as = Nihonbashi Hie Shrine, Sannō Otabi-sho |Fuda sites etc = |Religious affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine''' (日枝神社日本橋摂社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}} {{ill|Chūō, Tokyo|en|Chūō, Tokyo|WD=Q212704|lt=Chūō}} {{ill|Nihonbashi Kayabacho|ja|日本橋茅場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonbashi Kayabacho|1=Nihonbashi-Kayabachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508343}}. It is the external {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=Sessha}} of the {{ill|Hie Shrine|en|Hie Shrine|WD=Q702042|lt=Hie Shrine}} in {{ill|Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Chiyoda, Tokyo|WD=Q214051|lt=Chiyoda Ward}} {{ill|Nagatachō|en|Nagatachō|WD=Q1197269|lt=Nagatachō}}. It is also called '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine'''. ==History== In {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}}18th year ({{ill|1590|en|1590|WD=Q6764|lt=1590}}), following the entry of {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} into Edo, the {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Portable Shrine}} of Hie Shrine was enshrined here. The location of the enshrinement is called {{ill|Otabisho|en|Otabisho|WD=Q11489117|lt=Otabi-sho}}. Even today, during the Sannō Festival held every other year, the portable shrine and {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Dashi}} make a tour between the main shrine and this shrine[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php Venerable history and enshrined deities] Nihonbashi Hie Shrine{{Sfn|Masayoshi Kanayama|Rumi Kanayama|1993|p=63}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, {{ill|Chisenin|ja|智泉院 (東京都中央区)|lt=Chisenin|lt_ja=智泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chisenin|1=Chisen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106836711}} served as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) of this shrine; however, in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}}), it was detached from this shrine. Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *{{ill|Kayabachō Station|en|Kayabachō Station|WD=Q80566|lt=Kayabacho Station}} (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852387}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sannō Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is the external {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=Sessha}} of the {{ill|Hie Shrine|en|Hie Shrine|WD=Q702042|lt=Hie Shrine}} in {{ill|Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Chiyoda, Tokyo|WD=Q214051|lt=Chiyoda Ward}} {{ill|Nagatachō|en|Nagatachō|WD=Q1197269|lt=Nagatachō}}. It is also called '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine'''. ==History== In {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}}18th year ({{ill|1590|en|1590|WD=Q6764|lt=1590}}), following the entry of {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} into Edo, the {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Portable Shrine}} of Hie Shrine was enshrined here. The location of the enshrinement is called {{ill|Otabisho|en|Otabisho|WD=Q11489117|lt=Otabi-sho}}. Even today, during the Sannō Festival held every other year, the portable shrine and {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Dashi}} make a tour between the main shrine and this shrine[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php Venerable history and enshrined deities] Nihonbashi Hie Shrine{{Sfn|Masayoshi Kanayama|Rumi Kanayama|1993|p=63}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, {{ill|Chisenin|ja|智泉院 (東京都中央区)|lt=Chisenin|lt_ja=智泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chisenin|1=Chisen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106836711}} served as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) of this shrine; however, in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}}), it was detached from this shrine. Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *{{ill|Kayabachō Station|en|Kayabachō Station|WD=Q80566|lt=Kayabacho Station}} (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852387}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sannō Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The location of the enshrinement is called {{ill|Otabisho|en|Otabisho|WD=Q11489117|lt=Otabi-sho}}. Even today, during the Sannō Festival held every other year, the portable shrine and {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Dashi}} make a tour between the main shrine and this shrine[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php Venerable history and enshrined deities] Nihonbashi Hie Shrine{{Sfn|Masayoshi Kanayama|Rumi Kanayama|1993|p=63}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, {{ill|Chisenin|ja|智泉院 (東京都中央区)|lt=Chisenin|lt_ja=智泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chisenin|1=Chisen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106836711}} served as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) of this shrine; however, in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}}), it was detached from this shrine. Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *{{ill|Kayabachō Station|en|Kayabachō Station|WD=Q80566|lt=Kayabacho Station}} (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852387}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sannō Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

It is the external {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=Sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}} of the {{ill|Hie Shrine|en|Hie Shrine|WD=Q702042|lt=Hie Shrine|1=Hie Shrine}} in {{ill|Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Chiyoda, Tokyo|WD=Q214051|lt=Chiyoda Ward|1=Chiyoda, Tokyo}} {{ill|Nagatachō|en|Nagatachō|WD=Q1197269|lt=Nagatachō|1=Nagatachō}}. It is also called '''Nihonbashi Hie Shrine'''. ==History== In {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}}18th year ({{ill|1590|en|1590|WD=Q6764|lt=1590|1=1590}}), following the entry of {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} into Edo, the {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Portable Shrine|1=Mikoshi}} of Hie Shrine was enshrined here. The location of the enshrinement is called {{ill|Otabisho|en|Otabisho|WD=Q11489117|lt=Otabi-sho|1=Otabisho}}. Even today, during the Sannō Festival held every other year, the portable shrine and {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Dashi|1=Matsuri float}} make a tour between the main shrine and this shrine[http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/venerable.php Venerable history and enshrined deities] Nihonbashi Hie Shrine{{Sfn|Masayoshi Kanayama|Rumi Kanayama|1993|p=63}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, {{ill|Chisenin|ja|智泉院 (東京都中央区)|lt=Chisenin|lt_ja=智泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chisenin|1=Chisen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106836711}} served as the head temple ({{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) of this shrine; however, in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), it was detached from this shrine. Subsequently, with the elevation in status of the main Hie Shrine, this shrine was also reorganized. ==Access== *{{ill|Kayabachō Station|en|Kayabachō Station|WD=Q80566|lt=Kayabacho Station|1=Kayabachō Station}} (1 minute walk). ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * {{Cite book|Japanese book|author1=Masayoshi Kanayama|author2=Rumi Kanayama|authorlink1=Masayoshi Kanayama|authorlink2=Rumi Kanayama|title=Chūō Ward Historic Sites Walk|series=Tokyo Historic Sites Guide 2|publisher={{ill|Gakushusha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushusha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}|year=1993|ref=harv}} ==External Links== * {{Official website|http://www.hiejinjanihombashisessha.tokyo/ Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} - Official Homepage ** {{Instagram|nihombashihiejinja}} ** {{Twitter|sannouotabisho|Nihonbashi Hie Shrine}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|日枝神社日本橋摂社|version=101979565|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日枝神社日本橋摂社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852387}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Nihonbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sannō Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chuo, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]
